references.text:
[ErGr80] Erd\H{o}s, P. and Graham, R., Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
 
 [Gr64d] Graham, R. L., A property of Fibonacci numbers. Fibonacci Quart. (1964), 1-10.

remarks.text: Graham \cite{Gr64d} has shown that the sequence $a_n=F_n-(-1)^{n}$, where $F_n$ is the $n$th Fibonacci number, has these properties. Erd\H{o}s and Graham \cite{ErGr80} remark that it is easy to see that if $a_{n+1}/a_n>\frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}$ then the second property is automatically satisfied, and that it is not hard to construct very irregular sequences satisfying both properties.
